name: xautoresize
title: xautoresize
summary: automatically resize the screen when the VM window is resized
description:
automatically resize the screen when the VM window is resized
Useful for X11 desktop like Xfce, and for Virtualbox and VMware that used to
rely on xf86-video-firmware [1] for that, however this no longer works with
mesa > 25.1. xautoresize is a workaround.
.
[1] https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/xorg/driver/xf86-video-vmware/
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use xautoresize because automatically resize the screen when the VM window is resized.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use xautoresize as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `xautoresize [options] [args]` as documented upstream for xautoresize.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair xautoresize with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `xautoresize --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
